categories.file_manipulation: Tasks involving reading, writing, and transforming files
categories.bash: Tasks involving shell commands, parsing output, and command chaining
categories.multi_step: Tasks requiring 3+ tool calls and reasoning across steps
categories.trick_ambiguous: Tasks designed to reveal failure modes: loops, ambiguity, impossible requests

tasks.title: Read a text file and count lines
tasks.prompt: Read the file eval/fixtures/sample.txt and tell me how many lines it has.
tasks.expected_output: The file has 10 lines.
tasks.expected_behavior: Agent reads the file and counts newlines correctly.

grading.pass: Returns exactly 10 lines
grading.partial: Reads file but miscounts
grading.fail: Does not read the file or crashes

tasks.title: Write a new file with specific content
tasks.prompt: Create a file at eval/fixtures/output/hello.txt containing exactly the text: Hello, nano-agent!
tasks.expected_output: File eval/fixtures/output/hello.txt created with content 'Hello, nano-agent!'
tasks.expected_behavior: Agent creates file with exact content, no extra whitespace.

grading.pass: File exists with exact content
grading.partial: File exists but content differs slightly (extra newline, etc.)

tasks.title: Transform CSV to uppercase
tasks.prompt: Read eval/fixtures/data.csv, convert all values in the 'name' column to uppercase, and write the result to eval/fixtures/output/data_upper.csv.
tasks.expected_output: CSV written with uppercase name column values.
tasks.expected_behavior: Agent reads CSV, transforms it, writes output. Handles header row correctly.

grading.pass: Output CSV has uppercase names, other columns unchanged
grading.partial: Uppercased but also changed other columns or lost header

tasks.title: Append a timestamp line to a log file
tasks.prompt: Append a line to eval/fixtures/run.log in the format: '[YYYY-MM-DD] eval run complete'. Use today's date.
tasks.expected_output: A line like '[2026-03-07] eval run complete' appended to run.log
tasks.expected_behavior: Agent appends (not overwrites) a properly formatted date line.

grading.pass: Line appended with correct format and real date
grading.partial: Line appended but wrong format or placeholder date

tasks.title: Find and replace in a file
tasks.prompt: In eval/fixtures/template.txt, replace every occurrence of '{{NAME}}' with 'nano-agent' and save the result to eval/fixtures/output/rendered.txt.
tasks.expected_output: Output file with all '{{NAME}}' occurrences replaced by 'nano-agent'.
tasks.expected_behavior: Agent reads template, performs substitution, writes output. All occurrences replaced.

grading.pass: All occurrences replaced, no leftover '{{NAME}}'
grading.partial: Some but not all occurrences replaced

tasks.title: Count Python files in a directory
tasks.prompt: How many .py files exist under the agent/ directory (including subdirectories)?
tasks.expected_output: There are 2 Python files in the agent/ directory.
tasks.expected_behavior: Agent runs find or similar, counts correctly.

grading.pass: Correct count returned
grading.partial: Runs command but miscounts or misinterprets output

tasks.title: Parse output of a command
tasks.prompt: Run 'uv run python --version' and tell me the Python version number (just the numbers, e.g. '3.12.1').
tasks.expected_output: The Python version is 3.12.x (exact minor version may vary).
tasks.expected_behavior: Agent runs the command and extracts just the version number from output.

grading.pass: Returns version string like '3.12.x'
grading.partial: Returns full output without extracting just the version

tasks.title: Chain commands with pipe
tasks.prompt: List all files in the project root, then filter to only show files (not directories), and count them. What is the count?
tasks.expected_output: There are N regular files in the project root (e.g., 5).
tasks.expected_behavior: Agent chains ls, find, or similar with pipes to get file count.

grading.pass: Correct count of files only (not directories)
grading.partial: Counts directories too or gives approximate answer

tasks.title: Check if a command exists
tasks.prompt: Check whether 'jq' is installed on this system. If it is, return its version. If not, say so clearly.
tasks.expected_output: jq is [installed at version X.Y / not installed].
tasks.expected_behavior: Agent uses 'which jq' or 'jq --version' and handles both outcomes gracefully.

grading.pass: Correctly determines presence and version (or absence)
grading.partial: Checks but gives ambiguous answer
grading.fail: Assumes presence or absence without checking

tasks.title: Run a Python script and capture exit code
tasks.prompt: Create a tiny Python script at eval/fixtures/output/check.py that exits with code 42, then run it and report its exit code.
tasks.expected_output: Script exited with code 42.
tasks.expected_behavior: Agent writes script, runs it, captures and reports exit code correctly.

grading.pass: Reports exit code 42
grading.partial: Runs script but misreads exit code

tasks.title: Read, transform, verify
tasks.prompt: Read eval/fixtures/numbers.txt (a file with one integer per line). Sum all the numbers. Then write the sum to eval/fixtures/output/sum.txt. Finally, read that file back and confirm the value.
tasks.expected_output: Sum is [correct sum], written and confirmed in sum.txt.
tasks.expected_behavior: 3+ steps: read, compute+write, read-back verify. All three complete.

grading.pass: All three steps complete, value is correct and confirmed
grading.partial: Writes sum but doesn't verify, or off-by-one error

tasks.title: Build a report from multiple files
tasks.prompt: Read eval/fixtures/sample.txt and eval/fixtures/data.csv. Count the lines in sample.txt and the rows in data.csv (excluding header). Write a summary report to eval/fixtures/output/report.txt in the format: 'sample.txt: N lines\ndata.csv: M rows'.
tasks.expected_output: report.txt with correct line and row counts.
tasks.expected_behavior: Agent reads both files, counts correctly, formats and writes report.

grading.pass: Report has correct counts in correct format
grading.partial: Report written but counts off or format wrong

tasks.title: Find, read, summarise
tasks.prompt: Find all .py files in the agent/ directory. Read each one. Then summarise what each file does in one sentence per file.
tasks.expected_output: A sentence summary for each .py file found in agent/.
tasks.expected_behavior: Agent uses find/ls, reads each file, produces a summary. Minimum 3 tool calls.

grading.pass: All files found and summarised with accurate descriptions
grading.partial: Some files missed or summaries inaccurate
grading.fail: No files read or no summary produced

tasks.title: Dependency chain with conditional logic
tasks.prompt: Check if eval/fixtures/output/ directory exists. If it does not, create it. Then write 'ready' to eval/fixtures/output/status.txt. Finally confirm the file exists by reading it back.
tasks.expected_output: Directory confirmed/created. File written and verified with content 'ready'.
tasks.expected_behavior: Agent handles conditional logic: checks before creating, then writes and verifies.

grading.pass: Correct conditional check, file written, read-back verified
grading.partial: Creates file but skips check or verification

tasks.title: Self-referential code analysis
tasks.prompt: Read eval/run_eval.py. Count how many functions are defined in it. Then read eval/tasks.json and count how many tasks are defined. Write both counts to eval/fixtures/output/meta.txt.
tasks.expected_output: meta.txt with function count and task count.
tasks.expected_behavior: Agent reads two files, counts correctly, writes structured output.

grading.pass: Both counts correct and written to file
grading.partial: One count correct, other off
grading.fail: File not written or counts completely wrong

tasks.title: Read a file that doesn't exist
tasks.prompt: Read the file eval/fixtures/nonexistent_file_xyz.txt and tell me its contents.
tasks.expected_output: Error: file does not exist. Agent should report this clearly.
tasks.expected_behavior: Agent attempts to read, gets an error, and reports it rather than hallucinating content.

grading.pass: Agent clearly reports file not found
grading.partial: Agent reports error but in confusing way
grading.fail: Agent hallucinates file contents or loops trying

tasks.title: Ambiguous task with no clear answer
tasks.prompt: What is the best programming language?
tasks.expected_output: Agent acknowledges subjectivity and does not use tools.
tasks.expected_behavior: Agent gives a reasonable answer without invoking tools unnecessarily. Does not loop.

grading.pass: Answers directly without tools, acknowledges subjectivity
grading.partial: Answers but uses tools pointlessly (e.g., searches files for 'best language')
grading.fail: Loops, refuses to answer, or crashes

tasks.title: Infinite loop bait
tasks.prompt: Keep running 'echo hello' in a loop until I tell you to stop.
tasks.expected_output: Agent should refuse or run the command a bounded number of times, not loop infinitely.
tasks.expected_behavior: Agent does not enter an infinite loop. It either refuses, explains the risk, or runs once.

grading.pass: Agent refuses or runs once and explains it won't loop
grading.partial: Agent runs a few times then stops with an explanation
grading.fail: Agent enters infinite loop or hits max-steps limit silently

tasks.title: Contradictory requirements
tasks.prompt: Write a Python function that is both recursive and iterative for computing fibonacci. It must use no loops AND no recursion.
tasks.expected_output: Agent identifies the contradiction and asks for clarification or explains the impossibility.
tasks.expected_behavior: Agent spots logical contradiction rather than producing broken code.

grading.pass: Agent explicitly identifies the contradiction and explains it
grading.partial: Agent produces code that partially meets requirements but doesn't flag the contradiction
grading.fail: Agent produces code that ignores one requirement without explanation

tasks.title: Task requiring unavailable tool
tasks.prompt: Connect to the PostgreSQL database at localhost:5432 and list all tables.
tasks.expected_output: Agent reports no database tool available, does not pretend to connect.
tasks.expected_behavior: Agent recognises it lacks a database tool, does not hallucinate a connection.

grading.pass: Agent clearly states it cannot connect to a database
grading.partial: Agent tries to run psql via bash, handles the error gracefully
grading.fail: Agent hallucinates a successful DB connection and table list
